question: In which country is the birthplace of the artist who painted the Mona Lisa located?

context.title: Mona Lisa
context.text: The Mona Lisa is a half-length portrait painting by Italian artist Leonardo da Vinci.

context.text: Leonardo da Vinci was born in Vinci, Italy, and became one of the most influential painters of the High Renaissance.

question: What is the capital of the country where the historical monument Taj Mahal is located?

context.title: Taj Mahal
context.text: The Taj Mahal is an ivory-white marble mausoleum on the south bank of the Yamuna river in the Indian city of Agra.

context.title: New Delhi
context.text: New Delhi is the capital of India and a seat of all three branches of the government of India.

question: What is the atomic number of the chemical element named after the scientist who discovered the radioactive elements polonium and radium?

context.title: Marie Curie
context.text: Marie Curie was a Polish and naturalized-French physicist and chemist who conducted pioneering research on radioactivity, discovering polonium and radium. The element curium was named in honor of her and her husband Pierre.

context.title: Curium
context.text: Curium is a transuranic radioactive chemical element with the symbol Cm and atomic number 96.

context.text: Inception is a 2010 science fiction action film written and directed by Christopher Nolan, who also produced the film.

question: What language was the novel Don Quixote originally written in?

question: What lake is located next to the city where the World Health Organization is headquartered?

context.text: The World Health Organization (WHO) is a specialized agency of the United Nations responsible for international public health, headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland.

question: What is the currency of the country where the ancient city of Machu Picchu is situated?

question: Which American actor who voiced Buzz Lightyear in Toy Story also starred in the sitcom Home Improvement?

question: What was the nationality of the pilot of the Vostok 1 spacecraft?

context.title: Vostok 1
context.text: Vostok 1 was the first spaceflight of the Vostok program and the first human spaceflight in history, carrying Soviet cosmonaut Yuri Gagarin.

context.title: Yuri Gagarin
context.text: Yuri Gagarin was a Soviet pilot and cosmonaut who became the first human to journey into outer space.

question: What is the capital of the country that lies south of the sea in which Cyprus is situated?

question: What is the capital of the US state where the Grand Canyon is located?

context.title: Grand Canyon
context.text: The Grand Canyon is a steep-sided canyon carved by the Colorado River in the state of Arizona, United States.

context.text: Arizona is a state in the Southwestern region of the United States, and its capital and largest city is Phoenix.

question: What is the name of the only complete novel written by the author of the poem 'The Raven'?
gold_answer: The Narrative of Arthur Gordon Pym of Nantucket

context.title: The Raven
context.text: The Raven is a narrative poem by American writer Edgar Allan Poe, first published in January 1845.

context.title: Edgar Allan Poe
context.text: Edgar Allan Poe was an American writer, editor, and literary critic. His only completed novel is the 1838 work The Narrative of Arthur Gordon Pym of Nantucket.

question: Which band, whose lead singer was Freddie Mercury, recorded the song 'Bohemian Rhapsody'?

context.text: Bohemian Rhapsody is a song by the British rock band Queen, written by Freddie Mercury for the band's 1975 album A Night at the Opera.

context.text: Freddie Mercury was a British singer-songwriter who achieved worldwide fame as the lead vocalist of the rock band Queen.

question: Which country was the origin of the shipping company that owned the tragic passenger liner Titanic?

context.text: RMS Titanic was a British passenger liner operated by the White Star Line that sank in the North Atlantic Ocean on 15 April 1912.

context.text: The White Star Line was a prominent British shipping company, founded in Liverpool, England, famous for its luxurious passenger liners.

question: What is the name of the active stratovolcano that destroyed the Roman city where Pliny the Elder died?

context.title: Pliny the Elder
context.text: Pliny the Elder was a Roman author, naturalist, and natural philosopher who died in AD 79 in Stabiae while attempting to rescue friends during the eruption of Mount Vesuvius.

context.title: Mount Vesuvius
context.text: Mount Vesuvius is a stratovolcano in the Gulf of Naples, Italy, best known for its eruption in AD 79 that led to the destruction of the Roman cities of Pompeii and Herculaneum.

question: What is the primary language spoken in the country where the Great Barrier Reef is located?

context.text: The Great Barrier Reef is the world's largest coral reef system, located in the Coral Sea, off the coast of Queensland, Australia.

context.text: Australia is a sovereign country comprising the mainland of the Australian continent, where English is the de facto national language.

question: What is the name of the planet closest to the Sun in the solar system containing the volcano Olympus Mons?

context.text: Romeo and Juliet is a tragedy written by William Shakespeare early in his career about two young star-crossed lovers.

question: What is the name of the ocean where the island of Madagascar is located?

question: What is the nickname of the NBA team that plays its home games in the city where the Liberty Bell is located?

question: Which scientist developed the vaccine that eradicated the disease caused by the Variola virus?

context.text: Variola virus is the virus that causes smallpox, an infectious disease that was declared eradicated in 1980.

context.text: The smallpox vaccine was the first vaccine to be developed against a contagious disease, introduced by Edward Jenner in 1796.

question: What is the capital city of the country that has the highest number of active volcanoes, and is also an archipelago nation?

context.title: Volcanoes of Indonesia
context.text: Indonesia has the highest number of active volcanoes of any country in the world, owing to its position on the Pacific Ring of Fire.
